source.title: Structural elucidation of SARS-CoV-2 vital proteins: Computational methods reveal potential drug candidates against main protease, Nsp12 polymerase and Nsp13 helicase

gist_memory.paper_problem_memory: Urgent need for antivirals against SARS-CoV-2 due to COVID-19 pandemic. No licensed drugs or vaccines available. Computational drug discovery used as rapid alternative to high-throughput screening. Targets: conserved viral proteins Mpro, Nsp12 polymerase, Nsp13 helicase. Initial lack of crystal structures; homology models built. Later, crystal structure 6LU7 for Mpro became available and was used. Goal: identify small molecule inhibitors via virtual screening and MD simulations.

section_gists.heading: Structural elucidation of SARS-CoV-2 vital proteins: Computational methods reveal potential drug candidates against main protease, Nsp12 polymerase and Nsp13 helicase
ABSTRACT
section_gists.gist: Summarizes the problem (no antivirals for COVID-19), computational approach (virtual screening and MD simulations) to target Mpro, Nsp12, Nsp13, identification of binding modes and key residues, and lack of experimental validation.
process_signals: Computational methods used as alternative to high-throughput screening.
Study lacks experimental validation.

section_gists.heading: 1. Introduction
section_gists.gist: States the pandemic context, lack of approved drugs/vaccines, and rationale for targeting conserved non-structural proteins (Mpro, Nsp12, Nsp13). Notes initial absence of structural data, leading to homology modeling. Aims to identify hits via integrative VS and MD.
process_signals: No structural data available at study start; homology modeling used.
Conserved targets chosen for wide-spectrum inhibitor potential.
Computational study as starting point for drug discovery.

section_gists.heading: 2.1. Protein modeling
section_gists.gist: Homology models built with SWISSMODEL using high-identity templates (6NUR, 6JYT, 2Z9J). Later, crystal structure 6LU7 for Mpro used. Models refined by 20 ns MD. Binding pockets predicted by COACH and structure comparison.
process_signals: Initial design: homology modeling due to no crystal structures.
Revision: switched to crystal structure 6LU7 for Mpro when available.
Model refinement via MD to remove steric clashes.
metrics: RMSD of homology model vs crystal 6LU7: <0.5 Å

section_gists.heading: 2.2. Structure-based virtual screening and comparative docking
section_gists.gist: VS pipeline: ZINC database, Mcule platform, drug-like filters, diversity selection (Tanimoto). Docking with AutoDock Vina, then top hits re-docked with Glide XP. Grid centered on key binding residues.
process_signals: Diversity selection to maximize chemical space coverage.
Two-stage docking: Vina for screening, Glide XP for refinement.
Comparative docking to add reliability.
methods: Virtual screening (Mcule, drug-like filters, diversity selection)
Protein preparation (OPLS3 force field, hydrogen minimization)

section_gists.heading: 2.3. Molecular dynamics simulations and energy calculations
section_gists.gist: Best docked complexes subjected to 20 ns MD (AMBER18). MM-GBSA for binding free energy. Protocol from previous studies.
process_signals: MD used to validate docking poses and assess stability.
MM-GBSA for quantitative binding energy.

section_gists.heading: 3.1. Structural insights of SARS-CoV-2 proteins
section_gists.gist: Sequence analysis confirmed high similarity to SARS-CoV. Homology models validated (RMSD <1.35 Å, Ramachandran favored >96% for Mpro/RdRp, 84% for helicase). MD refinement improved models. Mpro model compared to 6LU7 (RMSD <0.5 Å).
process_signals: High sequence identity justified homology modeling.
Helicase model had lower Ramachandran favored (84.34%) and more outliers (4.43%), indicating potential quality issues.
Mpro model validated against crystal structure, leading to use of 6LU7.
metrics: RMSD: RdRp 1.35 Å, helicase 0.25 Å, Mpro 0.46 Å vs templates
failures_or_limits: Helicase model had lower quality (84.34% favored, 4.43% outliers).

section_gists.heading: 3.2. Identification of potential compounds against SARS-CoV-2 proteins
section_gists.gist: VS pipeline applied to each target. ADMET filtering removed hits with poor drug-likeness or toxicity. Top hits re-docked with Glide, then 20 ns MD. Stable complexes (last 5 ns) analyzed. Final hits: 5 Mpro, 4 Nsp12, 3 Nsp13. MM-GBSA energies calculated.
process_signals: ADMET filtering removed many hits (e.g., renal transporter inhibition, CYP450, high-risk groups like epoxides/quinones).
MD stability criterion: backbone RMSD stable in last 5 ns of 20 ns.
Final selection based on docking scores, MD stability, and MM-GBSA.
metrics: Final hit counts: Mpro 5, Nsp12 4, Nsp13 3
failures_or_limits: Many hits discarded due to poor ADMET (renal transporter, CYP450, high-risk groups).

section_gists.heading: 3.3. SARS-CoV-2 Mpro hits
section_gists.gist: Validation: redocked N3 peptide reproduced crystal pose. Hits filtered by interaction with catalytic dyad (His41/Cys145). 13 compounds MD simulated; 5 selected based on stability and MM-GBSA. cmp12, cmp14, cmp17, cmp18 had ΔGtol -41 to -45 kcal/mol. cmp17 had weak electrostatic energy (-5.39) due to unstable H-bonds. Key interactions with S1, S2, S4 subsites.
process_signals: Redocking validation successful.
Hit selection criterion: at least one H-bond with catalytic dyad or strong vdW.
cmp17 showed weak electrostatic contribution, indicating less favorable binding mode.
Consensus binding features observed for cmp12 and cmp14.
failures_or_limits: cmp17 had unstable H-bonds and weak electrostatic energy.

section_gists.heading: 3.4. SARS-CoV-2 Nsp12 RdRp
section_gists.gist: Hits selected based on H-bonds with conserved motifs, especially motif C (SDD). 17 hits MD simulated; 3 selected (cmp2, cmp17a, cmp21) with stable RMSD and favorable ΔGtol (-41.74, -34.37, -24.9). Key interactions with motifs A, B, C, D, F. Later, crystal structure 6M71 released; homology model RMSD 0.41 Å. Docking into 6M71 showed identical binding modes. Compared with remdesivir binding model.
process_signals: Selection focused on conserved polymerase motifs.
Later validation: crystal structure 6M71 confirmed model accuracy (RMSD 0.41 Å).
Binding modes consistent with known inhibitors (remdesivir).

section_gists.heading: 3.5. SARS-CoV-2 Nsp13 helicase
section_gists.gist: Hits selected based on interactions with six key NTPase residues. 20 ns MD on top complexes; 4 compounds identified. cmp3a fluctuated initially but adopted favorable conformation. MM-GBSA: cmp11 best (-49.73), cmp15 weakest (-30.56). Key residues: Lys288, Ser289, Asp374, Glu375, Gln404, Arg567. Per-residue decomposition highlighted Arg178, Ala312, Ala314.
process_signals: cmp3a showed initial instability but later stabilized.
cmp15 had least favorable binding energy.
Binding mode similar to Upf1-ADP.
metrics: ΔGtol: cmp1 -37.29, cmp3a -40.08, cmp11 -49.73, cmp15 -30.56 kcal/mol
failures_or_limits: cmp3a initial fluctuation; cmp15 weak binding energy.

section_gists.heading: 4. Discussion
section_gists.gist: Computational approach provides basis for inhibitor design. Conserved binding pockets support wide-spectrum inhibitors. Mpro hits interact with catalytic dyad and subsites; Nsp12 hits bind conserved motifs; Nsp13 hits mimic ADP binding. ADMET favorable, no high-risk groups. cmp2 is a known JAK inhibitor, may reduce inflammation. Study warrants in vitro testing.
process_signals: Emphasizes conserved features for wide-spectrum inhibitors.
cmp2 repurposing potential (JAK inhibitor).
Acknowledges need for experimental validation.

global_hypotheses_about_storyline: The study was conducted rapidly at the pandemic onset, starting with homology models due to lack of crystal structures, then adapting to newly released structures (6LU7, 6M71).
The workflow progressed from sequence analysis → homology modeling → virtual screening → docking refinement → MD validation → MM-GBSA ranking.
Target selection was based on conservation across coronaviruses, aiming for broad-spectrum potential.
ADMET filtering was a critical step that eliminated many initial hits.
MD simulations served as a dynamic filter to discard unstable complexes (e.g., cmp17 weak electrostatics, cmp3a initial fluctuation).
The study self-assesses as a starting point for experimental follow-up.
uncertainties_to_resolve: Were any hits discarded solely due to MD instability? The text mentions 13 Mpro compounds analyzed, but only 5 selected; similar for other targets.
How many initial hits were lost in ADMET filtering? Only qualitative mention.
What were the exact chemical structures of the final hits? Not fully described in text.
Were there any false positives in docking that MD revealed? Possibly cmp17 and cmp15.
How does the binding affinity compare to known inhibitors? Only remdesivir comparison mentioned for Nsp12.

events.summary: No licensed antiviral drugs or vaccines available for COVID-19, creating urgent need for drug discovery.
Computational drug discovery methods used as alternative to high-throughput screening for hit-to-lead optimization.
Later, co-crystallized Mpro structure (6LU7) used for docking instead of homology model.
Homology models refined by 20 ns MD simulations to remove steric clashes and optimize geometry.
Mpro homology model validated against crystal structure 6LU7, showing RMSD <0.5 Å.
Study lacks experimental validation of proposed hits.

events.summary: ADMET filtering removed many hits due to poor drug-likeness or toxicity.
Docking protocol validated by redocking N3 peptide into Mpro crystal structure.

Nsp12 hits selected by H-bonds with conserved motifs; 17 hits MD simulated, 3 selected with stable RMSD and favorable MM-GBSA.

chunk_events.notes: This chunk contains detailed results of virtual screening, MD simulations, and MM-GBSA analysis for all three targets, including validation steps and identification of final hits.

events.summary: Per-residue energy decomposition analysis for Nsp12 RdRp complexes identified major contributions from specific residues.
Discussion identifies conserved aspartate residues in Nsp12 motifs A and C that bind divalent metal ions, supported by mutational studies.
Discussion notes that Nsp13 helicase hits interact with conserved residues similar to ADP binding, with key contributions from Ser310, Lys288, Glu375, Arg178, Ala312, Ala314.
ADMET analysis shows identified compounds have promising properties, no high-risk groups, and are non-inhibitors of CYP450.
Study warrants in vitro testing to evaluate compound efficacy, acknowledging lack of experimental validation.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk contains discussion and analysis of results for all three targets, including per-residue energy decomposition, ADMET validation, and repurposing potential of cmp2. It also reiterates the need for experimental validation.
This chunk contains only reference list entries and no research process events.

evidence_bank.quote: To date, no licensed antiviral drugs or vaccines are available against COVID-19 although several clinical trials are under way to test possible therapies.
During this urgent situation, computational drug discovery methods provide an alternative to tiresome high-throughput screening, particularly in the hit-to-lead-optimization stage.
Later for molecular docking studies, a recently reported co-crystallized Mpro structure was utilized (PDB ID: 6LU7).
The generated homology models were refined using 20 ns molecular dynamics (MD) simulations to remove steric clashes and optimize side-chain geometry.

With the availability of the crystal structure of SARS-CoV-2 Mpro (6LU7) complexed with N3 peptide, homology model was also compared and less than 0.5 Å RMSD was found, which indicated the reliability of the generated model with only subtle difference

Although the current study lacks experimental validation, the structural information obtained from this computational study has paved way for the design of targeted inhibitors to combat COVID-19 outbreak.
A stepwise structure-based virtual screening pipeline was adopted considering the putative binding sites of SARS-CoV-2 Mpro, Nsp12 polymerase and Nsp13 helicase to identity potential hits
the subsequent screening resulted in 5, 4 and 3 hits for SARS-CoV-2 Mpro, Nsp12 and Nsp13, respectively
This filtering removed substantial hits that exhibited poor ADMET due to inhibitory effects on the renal organic cation transporter and CYP450 isozymes.
a large subset of hits was found to contain high-risk chemical groups like epoxides and quinones
As a validation docking protocol, the cocrystalized N3 peptide was removed and redocked with the substrate-binding site of SARS-CoV-2 Mpro (6LU7) using the same docking parameters.
Top-ranked SARS-CoV-2 Mpro hits were filtered based on the interaction with the catalytic dyad (at least one H-bond with either His41 or Cys145 or strong vdW interactions).
A total of 13 compounds were analysed after 20 ns MD simulation based on the stability for at least last 5 ns.
hits were selected based on establishing H-bond with conserved motifs, especially with signature motif C (SDD)
A total of 17 hits were analysed through 20ns MD simulations, and complexes with stable RMSD were further processed through MMGBSA binding free energy calculations.
the conformations of cmp2, cmp17a and cmp21 showed identical binding modes when docked inside the polymerase active site of crystal structure
top hits were selected based on significant interactions with six key residues (Lys288, Ser289, Asp374, Glu375, Gln404 and Arg567) including neighbouring residues involved in NTP hydrolysis
A 20 ns MD simulation on top ranked complexes led to the identification of 4 best compounds

Per-residue energy decomposition analysis indicated major contributions from Arg178 of domain 1B, Ala312 and Ala314 of domain 1A, which indicated < -1.5 kcal/mol binding energy with all four compounds.

functionally important aspartate residues of motif A (Asp623) and motif C (Asp760), together with conserved arginine residues of motif F (Arg553 and Arg555), and Ser759 (motif C) which were found to highly interact with the compounds.

In the active site, the side chains of Ser310, Lys288 and Glu375 actively participated in H-bond, while Arg178 and conserved alanine residues (Ala312 and Ala314) contributed substantially to total binding free energy.
the identified compounds exhibited promising ADMET properties and none of the compounds were found to contain any high-risk chemical group.

this comprehensive study presents an integrated computational approach to the identification of novel inhibitors into the area of anti-nCoV treatment as a starting point, which warrants in vitro testing to evaluate compound efficacy.
, Clinical pharmacology perspectives on the antiviral activity of azithromycin and use in COVID-19, Clin.
Hence, the cmp2 could be ideal for reducing the inflammatory activity during pathogenic COVID-19 epidemic, as lung inflammation has been observed during the SARS and MERS outbreaks.
The ongoing 2019–2020 outbreak of coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) [1], as of April 20, 2020, has claimed 157, 847 lives globally and has been declared as a pandemic.
, Structure of the RNA-dependent RNA Polymerase from COVID-19 Virus, Science 368 (2020) 779–782.
, Systematic review of the registered clinical trials of coronavirus diseases 2019 (COVID-19), medRxiv, 2020.

Recently emerged SARS-CoV-2 caused a major outbreak of coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) and instigated a widespread fear, threatening global health safety.
, Hydroxychloroquine and azithromycin as a treatment of COVID-19: results of an open-label non-randomized clinical trial, Int.
, Chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine as available weapons to fight COVID-19, Int.

Recently, hydroxychloroquine has shown to be effective in COVID-19 patients [7] and its efficiency has been reportedly reinforced by azithromycin for virus elimination [8], although no well-controlled, randomized clinical evidence supports azithromycin therapy in COVID-19 [9].
Department of Pharmaceutical and Pharmacological Sciences, Rega Institute for Medical Research, Medicinal Chemistry, University of Leuven, B-3000, Leuven, Belgium
Wang, Investigating interactions between HIV-1 gp41 and inhibitors by molecular dynamics simulation and MM–PBSA/GBSA calculations, J.

For SARS-CoV-2 Nsp13 helicase, top hits were selected based on significant interactions with six key residues (Lys288, Ser289, Asp374, Glu375, Gln404 and Arg567) including neighbouring residues involved in NTP hydrolysis $[42]$ .
, Inhibition of oncogenic kinases: an in vitro validated computational approach identified potential multi-target anticancer compounds, Biomolecules 9 (2019) 124.

Overall, SARS-CoV-2-Mpro hits were found to interact with catalytic dyad (His41 or Cys145) together with residues located at S4, S2 and S1 subsites of substrate binding pocket and mutational studies of His41 or Cys145 resulted in complete loss of enzymatic activity, which confirmed the role of catalytic dyad $[48,49]$ .

For SARS-CoV-2 Nsp12 RdRp, hits were selected based on establishing H-bond with conserved motifs, especially with signature motif C (SDD), which has been experimentally evaluated in altering polymerase activity supported by various mutational studies $[37–39]$ .
Model validation using PROCHECK indicated 96.51% (Mpro) and 97.

Moreover, Ivermectin, a broad spectrum anti-parasitic agent, was reported in vitro activity (\~5000-fold reduction) in a model of Vero/hSLAM cells infected with a SARS-CoV-2 isolate (Australia/VIC01/2020) [6].
Among these, motif A and C are most strikingly conserved aspartate residues that bind divalent metal ions necessary for catalytic activity $[30,31]$ .
Selected ligands ranked by AutoDock Vina were prepared using LigPrep as described previously $[23]$ .

Individual compounds of the docking library together with Mcule database were docked iteratively into respective binding pockets of the representative SARS-CoV-2 Mpro, Nsp12 polymerase, and Nsp13 helicase structures.

An automated in-built Autodock (AD) Vina docking engine was utilized to screen the composed docking library, which uses gradient optimization method in its local optimization process to efficiently rank the best poses $[22]$ .
The antechamber module of AMBER18 was used to generate atomic partial charges for the selected hits.
approved drugs has been reported, including remdesivir (GS-5734, Gilead) (EC $_{50}$ = 0.77 μM) and chloroquine (EC $_{50}$ = 1.

Computational drug discovery used as rapid alternative to high-throughput screening. Targets: conserved viral proteins Mpro, Nsp12 polymerase, Nsp13 helicase. Initial lack of crystal structures; homology models built. Later, crystal structure 6LU7 for Mpro became available and was used. Goal: identify small molecule inhibitors via virtual screening and MD simulations.
